title: [BUG] Cannot load an excel file with Error: Unexpected xml node in parseOpen: {"name":"lastModifiedBy","attributes":{},"isSelfClosing":false}
author: goforlee
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

<!-- A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->

Error: Unexpected xml node in parseOpen: {"name":"lastModifiedBy","attributes":{},"isSelfClosing":false}
    at CoreXform.parseOpen (...\node_modules\exceljs\lib\xlsx\xform\core\core-xform.js:82:15)
    at CoreXform.parse (...\node_modules\exceljs\lib\xlsx\xform\base-xform.js:63:16)
    at async XLSX.load (...\node_modules\exceljs\lib\xlsx\xlsx.js:355:36)

version "exceljs": "^4.4.0",

I found this error when I was trying to get images from an excel file. 
It only occurs with a few of my excel files. Other excel files work just fine.
All of my excel file has xlsx extension.

When I looked at core-xform.js, it has "cp:lastModifiedBy" but not just "lastModifiedBy". 
I dont think adding the missing key string in a map would be a solution for it in a long term...? I am not so sure. 
here is how the core.xml looks like
j
```
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<coreProperties xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/package/2006/metadata/core-properties" xmlns:cp="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/package/2006/metadata/core-properties" xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/" xmlns:dcterms="http://purl.org/dc/terms/" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
  <dcterms:created xsi:type="dcterms:W3CDTF">2016-08-03T05:32:42Z</dcterms:created>
  <dc:creator>Windows user</dc:creator>
  <lastModifiedBy>Windows user</lastModifiedBy>
  <lastPrinted>2017-08-02T03:34:29Z</lastPrinted>
  <dcterms:modified xsi:type="dcterms:W3CDTF">2017-08-02T04:16:14Z</dcterms:modified>
</coreProperties>

```
 
If anybody has a good idea to fix this issue, please help me!

## Steps To Reproduce

<!-- The exact steps required to reproduce the issue, ideally with a code example -->

```javascript
await workbook.xlsx.load(req.file.buffer);
```
